concept: Producing a JSON array from a spec and verifying field-type consistency across all objects
question: Create a JSON array of 4 book objects from this spec: each book has a string 'title', a string 'author', and an integer 'year'. Use: Dune/Herbert/1965, Neuromancer/Gibson/1984, Foundation/Asimov/1951, Beloved/Morrison/1987. Verify types are consistent.
solution: 1. Identify required fields and their types: title (string), author (string), year (integer). 2. Build object 1: {"title":"Dune", "author":"Herbert", "year":1965}. 3. Build object 2: {"title":"Neuromancer", "author":"Gibson", "year":1984}. 4. Build object 3: {"title":"Foundation", "author":"Asimov", "year":1951}. 5. Build object 4: {"title":"Beloved", "author":"Morrison", "year":1987}. 6. Check consistency: every 'title' is a string ✓, every 'author' is a string ✓, every 'year' is an integer (no quotes, no decimals) ✓. 7. Wrap in array brackets: the result is a valid JSON array of 4 consistent objects.
paraphrase: Step one, note the three required fields and their expected types: title and author are strings, year is an integer. Step two through five, construct one object per book: Dune by Herbert in 1965, Neuromancer by Gibson in 1984, Foundation by Asimov in 1951, and Beloved by Morrison in 1987. Step six, scan all four objects and confirm that every title value is enclosed in quotes (string), every author value is enclosed in quotes (string), and every year value is a bare whole number with no quotes or decimal point (integer). Step seven, place all four objects inside square brackets to form the final JSON array, which passes the type-consistency check.

concept: Producing a consistent JSON array of objects from a one-sentence specification
question: Build a JSON array of 4 objects, each with fields: name (string), age (integer), active (boolean). Use: Alice/30/true, Bob/25/false, Carlos/41/true, Diana/33/false.
solution: 1. Identify required fields: name must be a string, age must be an integer, active must be a boolean. 2. Build object 1: {"name":"Alice","age":30,"active":true}. 3. Build object 2: {"name":"Bob","age":25,"active":false}. 4. Build object 3: {"name":"Carlos","age":41,"active":true}. 5. Build object 4: {"name":"Diana","age":33,"active":false}. 6. Wrap all four in a top-level array. 7. Verify: every name is a quoted string, every age is an unquoted integer, every active is an unquoted boolean — types are consistent across all objects.
paraphrase: First, note the three required field types: name is textual (quoted), age is a whole number (unquoted), active is true/false (unquoted). Then construct each record in turn — Alice aged 30 who is active, Bob aged 25 who is inactive, Carlos aged 41 who is active, Diana aged 33 who is inactive. Place all four records inside square brackets to form the array. Finally, check every entry: all names appear as quoted strings, all ages appear as bare integers, and all active values appear as bare booleans, confirming the types are uniform throughout the array.
